channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Athletico-PR vence e afunda o Cruzeiro na lanterna do Brasileirão
item.description: Athletico-PR defeated Cruzeiro 2-1 in the 7th round of the 2026 Brasileirão, with goals from Stiven Mendoza and Viveros, while Villarreal scored for Cruzeiro. The win moved Paranaense to 7th place with 10 points, leaving Cruzeiro at the bottom with just 3 points.

item.title: Moro firma filiação ao PL para disputar governo do PR e sela volta à órbita do bolsonarismo
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro is expected to leave União Brasil and join the PL to run for governor of Paraná in 2026, after failing to gain support from his current party. The move marks a return to the bolsonarist orbit, with the PL aiming to use Moro as a platform for Flávio Bolsonaro’s presidential bid in Paraná and secure Bolsonarist influence in a state where Governor Ratinho Júnior (PSD) is also a presidential pre-candidate and may deny them local backing.

item.title: Já se deram conta que os tiros do Trump no Irã estão fazendo o diesel aumentar no mundo inteiro?, questiona Lula
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva criticized U.S. and Israeli attacks on Iran, stating that the global rise in diesel prices is a consequence of these actions and that countries like Brazil, which are not directly involved, are being unfairly burdened. He noted that Russia benefits from the situation due to eased restrictions on its oil sales and blamed the five permanent UN Security Council members for acting as 'owners of the world'. Lula warned that workers and the poor worldwide will be the main victims of this irresponsibility.

item.title: Tiros de Trump afetam o mundo e tem gente que se aproveita, diz Lula sobre alta nos combustíveis
item.description: President Lula blamed Donald Trump for raising global oil prices through attacks on Iran, which led to increased diesel and fuel costs in Brazil despite national self-sufficiency. He accused domestic actors of exploiting the crisis for profit and announced the suspension of PIS/Cofins taxes on fuels to reduce economic impact.

item.title: Deputado envia carta para Lula após aumento no valor da gasolina no DF
item.description: Distrital deputy Chico Vigilante (PT) sent a letter to President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva denouncing an alleged gas station cartel in the Federal District that raised gasoline prices from R$5.99 to R$6.55 and diesel to nearly R$10. He accuses stations of absorbing the PIS and COFINS tax exemption on diesel to boost profits instead of passing savings to consumers, and calls for reactivating CADE and Senacon, plus replacing technical staff with more combative profiles. Vigilante claims Lula responded by ordering the Justice Ministry and Federal Police to act against what he calls a 'brazen robbery'.

item.title: Lula diz que preço do combustível subiu porque tem 'gente que gosta de tirar proveito da desgraça'
item.description: President Lula blamed the rise in fuel prices in Brazil on individuals and entities exploiting global crises for profit, referencing the Iran conflict and the Strait of Hormuz as drivers of increased oil prices. He criticized the permanent members of the UN Security Council for financing wars instead of pursuing diplomacy, arguing that the burden falls on workers and the poor. Lula advocated for reforming the Security Council to make it more representative of the contemporary world.

item.title: Po, Ancelotti, e eu?, reage Neymar durante a convocação
item.description: Neymar reacted with humor and sadness to his omission from the Brazil national team squad announced by Ancelotti, jokingly asking 'Pô, Ancelotti, e eu?' while receiving treatment at Santos. He said he’s disappointed but has moved on, focusing on recovery to be ready for future opportunities, including a possible World Cup call-up. Ancelotti explained the omission was due to Neymar not being fully fit, but added that he could still make the World Cup if he reaches 100% fitness.

item.title: Moro decide se filiar ao PL para concorrer ao Governo do Paraná com o apoio de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro has decided to leave União Brasil and join the PL to run for Governor of Paraná in 2026, with the backing of Flávio Bolsonaro. The move followed a meeting with PL leadership, which pledged support to prevent Ratinho Jr. (PSD), a potential PSD presidential candidate, from dominating the state ticket. The PL also plans to nominate Filipe Barros for the Senate and offer the vice-governor slot to the União Brasil-PP federation.

item.title: Decreto do ECA Digital mira influenciadores mirins e práticas manipulativas de plataformas
item.description: President Lula signed the decree regulating the Digital ECA, expanding online protection for children and adolescents by targeting not only content but also platform design that influences user behavior. The decree bans monetization of content by influencers under 18 without judicial authorization and defines three types of 'manipulative practices' platforms must avoid, including obstructing user actions, exploiting cognitive vulnerabilities, and undermining rights like privacy controls. The ANPD will oversee enforcement, and the rules are now in effect.

item.title: Michelle quer novo encontro com Moraes para pedir que ele conceda domiciliar a Bolsonaro
item.description: Michelle Bolsonaro is seeking a new meeting with Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes to request house arrest for her husband, former President Jair Bolsonaro, citing the risk of bronchoaspiration at night and potential health deterioration if he remains incarcerated. She claims that, according to medical staff, if help had arrived one hour later, Bolsonaro could have died during the episode that led to his hospitalization. Bolsonaro remains hospitalized with bacterial bronchopneumonia, resulting from vomit aspiration linked to the sequelae of the 2018 stabbing.

item.title: Cuba sob o cerco de Trump: as toneladas de comida e remédios que governo Lula envia à ilha sem alarde
item.description: The Lula government is preparing a humanitarian donation of approximately 21,000 tons of food and medicine for Cuba in response to the island's worsening economic and energy crisis. The aid, requested by Havana, is being handled discreetly to avoid politicization during an election year and depends on Cuba making a vessel available for transport. If confirmed, it would be Brazil's largest single-country donation in the second half of 2025, surpassing the total aid sent to 22 countries across Latin America, the Caribbean, Africa, Asia, and Europe in the same period.

item.title: Fux suspende regras de eleição indireta para mandato-tampão no Rio
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Luiz Fux suspended parts of Rio de Janeiro's state law that mandated open, nominal, and in-person voting in the state legislature for an indirect gubernatorial election, as well as a 24-hour deadline for candidates to resign their posts. The ruling, granted in response to a PSD petition citing unconstitutionality, cited concerns over criminal group influence and the insufficiency of the short timeline to ensure electoral fairness.

item.title: Fabiana Bolsonaro faz “blackface” em protesto contra Erika Hilton
item.description: São Paulo state deputy Fabiana Bolsonaro (PL) performed a blackface act during an Alesp session on March 18, 2026, painting her face brown to protest the appointment of federal deputy Erika Hilton (PSOL-SP) to lead the Chamber’s Women’s Rights Defense Committee. She claimed, mockingly, that she now ‘identified’ as Black and questioned why she couldn’t chair an anti-racism commission, prompting accusations of transphobia, racism, and hate speech from PSOL deputy Monica Seixas, who demanded the session be suspended and Fabiana be held ethically and legally accountable.

item.title: Fux suspende regras da Alerj para eleição indireta para governo do Rio
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Luiz Fux suspended parts of Rio de Janeiro's state law that changed rules for the indirect election of governor and vice-governor, including open, nominal, and in-person voting in Alerj and reducing the incompatibility period to 24 hours. The decision responded to a request from the PSD, which argued the changes were unconstitutional, with Fux stressing the need to uphold constitutional rules amid an imminent vacancy. The indirect election will follow the departure of Vice Governor Thiago Pampolha to the State Court of Accounts and Governor Cláudio Castro, who is stepping down to run for the Senate in October.

item.title: As cúpulas militares estão preocupadas com a crise institucional, mas se consideram fora dela
item.description: Lula asked military commanders about troop opinions on the STF following the Master scandal. The military responded by demanding equal rigor in investigations, prosecution, and punishment against STF members, suggesting house arrest for Alexandre de Moraes and Jair Bolsonaro. They expressed concern over declining public trust in the STF and geopolitical dilemmas from U.S. pressure and military inadequacy, maintaining a 'silent pact' in international relations while pursuing defense partnerships with Turkey, India, and the U.S.

item.title: Durigan está encaminhado como ministro, diz Haddad
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad stated that executive secretary Dario Durigan is set to replace him, with the announcement expected from President Lula. Haddad is stepping down this week to run for governor of São Paulo in the October elections, having accepted Lula’s request to run despite earlier reluctance. His departure will be published in the Official Gazette by Friday, March 20, and while the campaign team and headquarters are ready, the PT has yet to finalize its vice-gubernatorial candidate and second Senate nominee in the São Paulo alliance.

item.title: RJ: Fux suspende regras para eleição indireta aprovada para mandato-tampão
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Luiz Fux suspended parts of a rule approved by Rio de Janeiro state deputies that allowed indirect elections for temporary mandates. The decision aims to uphold constitutional integrity in the state's electoral process, preventing potential manipulations in gubernatorial succession. The suspension is provisional, pending a final ruling by the Court's plenary.

item.title: Davi rebate Valdemar Costa Neto sobre veto à dosimetria e CPI do Master
item.description: Senate President Davi Alcolumbre denied allegations by PL party leader Valdemar Costa Neto that he had sought a deal to block the Banco Master CPI in exchange for overturning the presidential veto on the Dosimetry Bill. Alcolumbre said he was stunned by the claims and clarified there is no set date for Congress to review the veto, which Lula issued to prevent reduced sentences for those convicted in the 2022 and 2023 coup attempts. Allies of Bolsonaro are pushing for the veto to be overturned.

item.title: Moro quer presidente da Fiep como vice em eventual governo no PR
item.description: Sergio Moro, the pré-candidate for governor of Paraná from União Brasil, has proposed Edson Vasconcelos, president of Fiep, as his potential running mate, but faces an obstacle: Vasconcelos is not affiliated with the PP, the party in the federation with União Brasil. Moro has already agreed on his candidacy with PP president Ciro Nogueira and leads in voter intention polls in Paraná, according to a Paraná Pesquisas survey from January.

item.title: Fux altera regras de eleição indireta no RJ e atinge pré-candidatos a mandato-tampão
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Luiz Fux annulled two rules for the indirect election in Rio de Janeiro, mandating the six-month incompatibility period and secret voting by deputies. The decision impacts pre-candidates for the interim governorship, including state secretaries and allies of Governor Cláudio Castro, who sought to run after his expected resignation to pursue a Senate seat. The injunction was granted in response to a request from the PSD, the party of Rio Mayor Eduardo Paes, who is a pre-candidate in the October direct election.

item.title: Sergio Moro chega ao PL: relembre as brigas com a família Bolsonaro
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro (União-PR) will join the PL to bolster Flávio Bolsonaro’s gubernatorial bid in Paraná for 2026, despite his past conflicts with the Bolsonaro family, including accusations of political interference in the Federal Police in 2020 and criticism of the government’s vaccine hesitancy during the pandemic. His return follows a period of reconciliation, but the STF has ordered the reopening of an investigation into alleged obstruction of justice by Bolsonaro concerning the 2020 dismissal of Federal Police director Maurício Valeixo.

item.title: PT diz que volta de nomes da velha guarda ao Congresso melhora debate
item.description: The national president of the Workers' Party (PT), Edinho Silva, states that the return of experienced party figures like José Dirceu and João Paulo Cunha to Congress improves debate and opinion formation in the plenary. Both are expected to run for federal deputy seats from São Paulo, with high hopes for Dirceu’s vote count, which could help the PT elect more legislators. Dirceu, former Chief of Staff, and Cunha, former Chamber president, had their Lava Jato convictions annulled and have regained political rights.

item.title: Lula quer discutir combate ao crime organizado na Celac
item.description: President Lula will travel to Bogotá to attend the 10th CELAC summit, where he will discuss combating organized crime from a penitentiary administration perspective, avoiding the labeling of criminal groups as 'terrorists' due to lack of consensus among members and concerns over financial sanctions. The meeting will also include the first CELAC-Africa High-Level Forum, focusing on Brazilian agricultural exports to Africa. Additional topics include economic development, hunger, poverty, climate change, and food security.

item.title: Avanço de Flávio era esperado no início de sua pré-candidatura, diz PT
item.description: PT president Edinho Silva states that Flávio Bolsonaro's rise in the polls was expected due to Brazil's strong political polarization, arguing that any candidate associated with Bolsonaro's legacy would gain rapid support. He adds that Lula, as president, must focus on governing rather than engaging in opposition dynamics, and that the current polarized environment makes a strong third-way candidacy unlikely in 2026.

item.title: Haddad: Amanhã sou ministro ainda, tenho agenda com presidente em SP
item.description: Fernando Haddad announced that he will still have ministerial duties on Thursday, accompanying President Lula in São Paulo for events including the opening of the 17ª Caravana Federativa, the awarding of an honorary doctorate to Pepe Mujica, and a press statement at the Metalworkers’ Union of São Bernardo do Campo, where he is expected to announce his candidacy for governor of São Paulo. Haddad suggested that Dario Durigan, the current executive secretary, will succeed him at the Finance Ministry, though he deferred the official announcement to the president. He also declined to comment on the Copom’s recent Selic rate cut.

item.title: Democracia dos EUA está se deteriorando a uma velocidade sem precedentes, diz relatório global
item.description: A V-Dem report states that U.S. democracy is deteriorating at an unprecedented speed, with its Liberal Democracy Index score dropping 24% in one year and its global ranking falling from 20th to 51st out of 179 nations. The country has been reclassified from a 'liberal democracy' to an 'electoral democracy' due to the Trump administration's consolidation of power, erosion of civil liberties, politicization of institutions, and attacks on the press and judiciary.

item.title: Amiga de Lulinha recebeu R$ 4,1 milhões de empresário do esquema que derrubou ministro, aponta Coaf
item.description: Roberta Luchsinger, a close friend of President Lula’s eldest son, received R$ 4.1 million from companies linked to Eduardo DP, a Maranhão businessman implicated in procurement fraud and previously arrested in the operation that led to the indictment of former Communications Minister Juscelino Filho. The funds were transferred by EDP Infraestrutura e Pavimentação to RL Consultoria, Roberta’s firm, which claims the payments were for legitimate private services unrelated to the INSS investigation. Lulinha’s and Roberta’s legal teams denounced the probes as a 'fishing expedition' and denied any involvement by Fábio Lula in the transactions.

item.title: Lulinha se predispõe a prestar depoimento para Mendonça
item.description: Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, has made himself available to Minister André Mendonça to testify in the investigation into irregularities in INSS associative discounts. His defense denies any wrongdoing in his relationship with Antonio Carlos Camilo, the 'Careca do INSS', stating that a trip to Portugal was intended to explore business opportunities in cannabidiol products before the businessman was under investigation. Mendonça has authorized the breaking of Lulinha’s fiscal secrecy and is cross-referencing data with other suspects to trace financial flows.

item.title: Justiça Eleitoral pelo Brasil: TRE-SC realiza mais de 3 mil atendimentos itinerantes no início de 2026
item.description: The Regional Electoral Court of Santa Catarina (TRE-SC) conducted 3,032 mobile electoral services between January and February 2026 across 20 municipalities under the 'Justiça Eleitoral em Movimento' program. These actions aim to expand access to electoral services in areas without fixed electoral offices or with limited mobility. In the 2025-2026 cycle, the program has already delivered 21,202 services across 170 municipalities, emphasizing the need for voters to regularize their electoral registration before the May 6, 2026 deadline.

item.title: Atritos entre governadores e seus vices mudam cenário eleitoral em estados
item.description: In at least five states (Alagoas, Amazonas, Maranhão, Rio Grande do Norte, and Tocantins), governors barred from seeking re-election are choosing to remain in office until January 2027, forgoing Senate runs due to political conflicts with their vice governors and struggles over control of state machinery. This breaks a long-standing Brazilian political tradition where governors typically step aside to let their successors run for re-election. In Rio Grande do Norte, Governor Fátima Bezerra (PT), who was expected to run for Senate with Lula’s support, has opted to stay in office, significantly altering both state and national electoral dynamics.

item.title: Senado aprova reajuste para integrantes do Ministério Público da União, aumento custará R$ 1,6 bi até 2028
item.description: The Federal Senate approved a salary adjustment for the Union's Public Ministry (MPU), with an impact of R$ 1.6 billion by 2028, along with the creation of positions in the CNJ and DPU. The MPU increase provides for an 8% annual raise from 2026 to 2028 for effective, commissioned, and trust-based positions. The bills now await sanction by President Lula.

item.title: Eca Digital é uma das legislações mais avançadas do mundo, diz Lula
item.description: President Lula praised the Digital ECA as one of the world’s most advanced laws for protecting children and adolescents online, during the signing of decrees regulating the law. He stressed that the law alone is not enough and called on civil society to monitor and demand action against risks such as sexual exploitation of minors on social media, citing UNICEF data showing that 1 in 5 Brazilian children suffered internet-facilitated sexual abuse in one year.

item.title: Aracaju aposta em projeto no Congresso para ganhar disputa por território com cidade vizinha
item.description: The mayor's office of Aracaju is advancing a complementary bill in Congress that conditions municipal dismemberment on a popular referendum and feasibility study, aiming to resolve a 20 km² territorial dispute with São Cristóvão. Approved by the Chamber and now under Senate review, the proposal seeks to overturn a judicial ruling that ordered the return of the area to the neighboring city, with Sergipe's congressional bloc pushing for swift approval ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Caminhoneiros mantêm ameaça de greve enquanto avaliam medidas do governo
item.description: Truckers are maintaining a strike threat while awaiting the official publication of government measures to regulate freight prices, including increased ANTT enforcement and a possible temporary ICMS exemption on diesel by states, with 50% compensation from the federal government. A final decision on whether to proceed with the strike will be made starting tomorrow, following evaluations of the announcements made in Santos today.

item.title: Moro acerta data de filiação ao partido da família Bolsonaro para concorrer ao governo do Paraná
item.description: Senator Sergio Moro has confirmed his departure from União Brasil and upcoming affiliation with the PL, the party of the Bolsonaro family, to run for governor of Paraná in 2026. The announcement was made by PL national president Valdemar Costa Neto, who stated that Moro's affiliation will take place next Tuesday (the 24th).

item.title: Copom reduz Selic para 14,75%, mas Brasil tem a 2ª maior taxa real do mundo
item.description: The Copom cut the Selic rate to 14.75% per year, marking the first reduction since May 2024, yet Brazil retained the world's second-highest real interest rate at 9.51%, behind only Turkey. The decision was unanimous, though the Committee operated with two fewer directors after the mandates of Renato Dias de Brito Gomes and Diogo Abry Guillen ended on December 31, 2025. Inflation projections for end-2026 rose to 4.1%, driven by oil price surges linked to the Middle East war.

item.title: Uczai: governo federal é o mais aliado dos caminhoneiros
item.description: Pedro Uczai (PT-SC), leader of the Workers' Party in the Chamber of Deputies, stated that the federal government is the 'greatest ally of truckers' and urged governors to reduce the ICMS tax on fuels. He highlighted that the Lula administration has already acted by eliminating PIS and Cofins, increasing export taxes, and adding a 32-cent subsidy, while criticizing Bolsonaro's government for refinery privatizations and international price parity. Uczai also warned of potential Bolsonarist influence in trucker mobilizations but stressed there is no fuel shortage risk and that truckers should collaborate on solutions.

item.title: Nomeação de aprovados para Justiça Eleitoral é necessária, afirma Zequinha
item.description: Senator Zequinha Marinho (Podemos-PA) advocated for the approval of PL 4/2024, which would appoint 474 candidates who passed public competitions to the Electoral Justice, stating it is essential to ensure the functioning of the electoral system ahead of the 2026 elections. He warned that the return of seconded employees to their original offices could reduce the Electoral Justice workforce by up to one-third, threatening the voter registration process ending May 6. The senator stressed that immediate appointments are indispensable to guarantee the organization, security, and transparency of the electoral process, noting that budget and resources are available — only legal authorization is pending.

item.title: VÍDEO: Fabiana Bolsonaro faz ‘blackface’ para atacar mulheres trans na Alesp
item.description: State deputy Fabiana Bolsonaro (PL-SP) performed blackface during a speech at Alesp to attack transgender women, particularly in response to Erika Hilton’s (Psol) election as president of the Women’s Rights Defense Committee. The act was widely condemned as racist and transphobic, sparking immediate backlash in the chamber and on social media, with calls for ethical sanctions and potential legal action. Despite her surname, she is not related to Jair Bolsonaro, having adopted the name strategically in 2022.

item.title: Deputados de oposição querem que Lula proponha demissão de Gonet da PGR por omissão
item.description: Opposition deputies Caroline de Toni (PL-SC) and Gustavo Gayer (PL-GO) sent a letter to President Lula requesting the dismissal of Attorney General Paulo Gonet over alleged omissions in investigations involving the Master Bank and Supreme Court ministers Dias Toffoli and Alexandre de Moraes. They claim Gonet archived requests to assess Toffoli’s potential conflict of interest and failed to act on allegations against Moraes, invoking Article 128 of the Constitution, which requires an absolute Senate majority for removal.

item.title: Alcolumbre rebate Valdemar Costa Neto e diz que presidente do PL ‘mente reiteradamente’
item.description: Senate President Davi Alcolumbre rejected accusations from PL leader Valdemar Costa Neto that he had negotiated a deal to block the Banco Master CPI in exchange for overturning the veto on amnesty for January 8 defendants. Alcolumbre said he was 'astonished' by the claims, denied any negotiations, and suggested Valdemar suffers from mythomania, asserting he lies repeatedly.

item.title: Abin alerta para risco de enquadrar PCC e CV como terroristas
item.description: The Abin warns that labeling PCC and CV as terrorist organizations by the U.S. could trigger international sanctions and increase foreign interference in Brazil. The internal assessment was sent to President Lula, highlighting threats to national sovereignty.

item.title: Congresso aprova uso imediato de tornozeleira eletrônica por agressor de mulher
item.description: The Senate approved a bill allowing immediate use of electronic ankle monitors for aggressors against women as an urgent protective measure under the Maria da Penha Law, with police oversight and a victim alert device. The proposal, already passed by the Chamber of Deputies, awaits President Lula’s sanction. The measure is prioritized in cases of prior violation of protective orders or imminent risk to the victim’s physical or psychological integrity.

item.title: Caiado diz ter sofrido bullying por cantar mal
item.description: The governor of Goiás and pre-candidate for president, Ronaldo Caiado (PSD), said he suffered bullying in childhood for singing poorly, being barred from the first communion choir by a nun. Despite this, he continues to sing in public events and on social media, such as during the 2026 Water Week in Goiânia, where he performed 'Tocando em Frente' with a sertanejo duo. Caiado also highlighted the Aprendiz do Futuro program, which has served 8,5 thousand young people and invested over R$ 453 million since 2021.

item.title: Zema critica STF e diz que 'é como se nós tivéssemos um papa pedófilo'
item.description: Governor of Minas Gerais, Romeu Zema (Novo), a pre-candidate for president, criticized the Federal Supreme Court (STF), stating that the ministers' conduct would be like 'having a pedophile pope' influencing priests. The remarks were made on Wednesday, the 18th, and sparked strong backlash due to their aggressive tone and inflammatory analogy.

item.title: Mujica receberá título doutor honoris causa da Universidade do ABC
item.description: Former Uruguayan president José 'Pepe' Mujica, who died in March 2025 at age 89, will posthumously receive the title of Doctor Honoris Causa from the Federal University of ABC (UFABC) in a ceremony scheduled for March 19, 2026. The honor was approved by the University Council in June 2024, one year before his death, recognizing his legacy as a Latin American left-wing icon and advocate for democracy, education, ethics, and regional integration. The event will be attended by his companion Lucía Topolansky and Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva.

item.title: Último vídeo da websérie “V de Verdade” alerta: desconfie de conteúdos que possam parecer artificiais
item.description: The sixth episode of the TSE's 'V de Verdade' webseries warns about verisimilitude — when artificial content mimics reality to deceive and influence electoral decisions. It highlights how convincing headlines, edited images, and AI-generated material are widespread on social media, urging users to verify information before sharing.

item.title: As novas derrotas de Pablo Marçal no TSE
item.description: Political commentator Pablo Marçal suffered two defeats at the Superior Electoral Court (TSE) after Minister Cármen Lúcia rejected extraordinary appeals against fines of R$30,000 and R$10,000 for unfounded attacks during the 2024 municipal elections. The penalties stemmed from false claims that São Paulo Mayor Ricardo Nunes 'abuses women and doesn’t admit it' and that federal deputy Tabata Amaral 'would do the M off,' implying nonexistent support. Marçal may still appeal to the Supreme Federal Court (STF).

item.title: Governo avalia pedir indenização contra distribuidoras e postos para conter preço do diesel
item.description: The government is considering seeking collective moral and material damages from fuel distributors and stations over abusive diesel price increases, with the AGU leading the legal action and the PF investigating alleged speculative movements. It also proposes exempting ICMS on diesel imports until May, with the federal government compensating half of states' losses, and plans stricter freight inspections for truckers, aiming to mitigate potential political fallout ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Alta nas pesquisas faz Flávio Bolsonaro adiar divulgaçăo de plano de governo
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) postponed the release of his government plan for the presidency, initially scheduled for March 30 in São Paulo, due to rising poll numbers and to prevent opponents from using his proposals as attack points. The document, being prepared for five months with consultancy from GO Associados, covers fiscal economy, education, water security, and indigenous lands, and may be moved to the candidacy registration period (July 20 to August 5).
